explanation: This command simulates the malicious behavior described in the Sigma rule by using ping to delay execution and del to delete a file. It uses standard syntax and no obfuscation to remain a valid test case.

This command adds explicit quotes around the command parts, which is a safe mutation technique. It maintains the same behavior as the original but uses spacing and quotation changes to test detection resilience.
This command reorders the del flags (/f /q to /q /f), which is a valid mutation technique. The command behavior remains unchanged, but the argument order is altered for testing purposes.
This command uses minimal spacing and no unnecessary characters, representing a simplified version of the original command. It tests whether the detection rule is sensitive to spacing variations.
